name: Areas Related to Circles — Sectors and Segments
whatIsIt: A whole circle has area πr² and boundary (circumference) 2πr. A SECTOR is a pizza-slice piece with an angle θ at the centre: its area is (θ/360) × πr² and its curved arc length is (θ/360) × 2πr. A SEGMENT is the region between a chord and the arc: segment area = sector area − triangle area.
whyNeeded: Real round things are often used in parts: a slice of a circular field to irrigate, the metal in a fan's swing, the area a windscreen wiper cleans, or the crust length of a pizza slice. Sector and segment formulas measure exactly the part you use, not the whole circle.
realLifeUses: Area a windscreen wiper or fan sweeps (a sector)
Amount of pizza or cake in one slice
Length of fencing along a curved garden edge (arc length)
Land in a pie-slice plot between two boundary roads

vocabulary.meaning: A pizza-slice part of a circle bounded by two radii and an arc; area = (θ/360) × πr².
The curved edge of a sector: (θ/360) × 2πr.
The region between a chord and its arc; area = sector area − triangle area.
The angle the sector makes at the centre, in degrees.

story.title: Fatima's Fan Slice
story.text: A table fan blade sweeps a quarter turn (90°) with a reach of 14 cm. 'How much area does it fan?' Fatima asks. Abbu says a quarter of a circle is a sector with θ = 90°. Area = (90/360) × (22/7) × 14² = ¼ × (22/7) × 196 = ¼ × 616 = 154 cm². 'One quarter of the whole circle's area,' he nods.
story.extractedProblem: A sector has angle 90° and radius 14 cm. Find its area (π = 22/7).

props.label: a 90° sector = one quarter of the circle

visual.caption: A 90° sector is one quarter of the pizza: its area is ¼ of πr², and the crust is ¼ of the circumference.

teachingGallery.title: Fraction of the whole circle
teachingGallery.note: A sector is θ/360 of the circle — use that fraction of πr² for area and of 2πr for arc.

examples.caption: θ = 180° is half: area = ½ πr².
A segment is the sector with its triangle removed.

standardMethod.summary: Write the fraction θ/360, then multiply by πr² for sector area or by 2πr for arc length. For a segment, subtract the triangle's area from the sector's.
steps: Find the fraction of the circle: θ ÷ 360.
Sector area = (θ/360) × πr². Use π = 22/7 or 3.14.
Segment area = sector area − area of the triangle formed by the two radii and the chord.
Check units: area in square units, arc length in length units.

workedExamples.problem: Find the area of a 90° sector of radius 14 cm (π = 22/7).
Find the arc length of a 90° sector of radius 7 cm (π = 22/7).

commonMistakes.mistake: Using the whole πr² for a sector instead of the θ/360 fraction.
commonMistakes.fix: A sector is only PART of the circle. Multiply πr² by θ/360 — a 90° sector is just ¼ of the whole.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using πr² (an area formula) for arc LENGTH.
commonMistakes.fix: Arc length uses the circumference 2πr (a length), not the area πr². Take θ/360 of 2πr.

easy.q: A 90° sector is what fraction of a full circle? Write the bottom number of the fraction 90/360 in lowest terms n/? — just give the denominator.
Area of a full circle with r = 7 (π = 22/7): 22/7 × 49 = ?

medium.q: Area of a 90° sector, r = 14 (π = 22/7): ¼ × 616 = ?
Arc length of a 90° sector, r = 7 (π = 22/7): ¼ × 44 = ?

challenge.q: Area of a 180° (half) sector, r = 7 (π = 22/7): ½ × 154 = ?
For arc length of a sector, Meena uses πr². What's the fix?

options.label: πr² is right for arc length

hintLadder: Arc is a length, not an area.

questions.q: A 60° sector is 60/360 = 1/? of the circle — give the denominator.
Arc length of a 90° sector, r = 7 (π = 22/7)?

options.label: Sector area minus the triangle area

questions.answer: Sector area minus the triangle area
hintLadder: A segment is a sector with its triangle cut off.

teachBackPrompt: Cut a paper circle into a quarter-slice for a grown-up and show it is a 90° sector — one quarter of πr² for area and one quarter of 2πr for the curved crust. Explain that a segment is a slice with its triangle removed.

revisionCard.summary: Full circle: area πr², circumference 2πr. Sector (angle θ): area = (θ/360)πr², arc length = (θ/360)2πr. Segment = sector − triangle. Always multiply by the fraction θ/360, and use 2πr (not πr²) for arc LENGTH.

rememberIt.hook: Sector = pizza slice. Area = (angle/360) x pi r^2
rememberIt.unpack: Arc length = (angle/360) x 2 pi r. A sector is a fraction of the whole circle - the fraction is angle/360.

studentQuestions.q: How do I find a sector's area?
studentQuestions.a: Take its fraction of the circle (its angle out of 360) times the full area pi r^2.
Sector = slice between two radii (pizza slice). Segment = the piece cut off by a chord (the crust).
